metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC1 to explicitly verify that the `import _call_with_backoff` statement in `acli.py` is also removed (not just its `__all__` entry).
findings.finding: AC1 verifies removal from `acli_subprocess.py` and `acli.py __all__`, but does not explicitly cover removal of the import statement in `acli.py` that imports `_call_with_backoff`. If the import line is left in place after the symbol is deleted, `acli.py` will have a broken import that AC1 does not catch.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC1 to: '`_call_with_backoff` is removed from `acli_subprocess.py`, its import statement is removed from `acli.py`, and it is absent from `acli.py __all__`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the ACs to cover removal of the `import random`, `import urllib.error` module-level imports, and the `_RETRYABLE_HTTP_CODES` constant from `acli_subprocess.py`, plus its corresponding import and `__all__` entry in `acli.py`, all of which become orphaned once `_call_with_backoff` is deleted.
findings.finding: Deleting `_call_with_backoff` orphans three artifacts in acli_subprocess.py that are used exclusively by it — `import random` (line 15), `import urllib.error` (line 19), and `_RETRYABLE_HTTP_CODES` (line 112) — plus `_RETRYABLE_HTTP_CODES`'s import and `__all__` entry in acli.py (lines 51, 95). The plan's ACs name only `_call_with_backoff` and its `__all__` entry, leaving these as dead code post-deletion that will trigger F401 linter errors and continued API surface pollution.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / acli_subprocess.py lines 15, 19, 112 / acli.py lines 51, 95
findings.suggested_fix: Add a fourth AC: '`import random`, `import urllib.error`, and `_RETRYABLE_HTTP_CODES` are removed from `acli_subprocess.py`; `_RETRYABLE_HTTP_CODES` is also removed from `acli.py`'s import block and `__all__`.' Verify with `grep -n _RETRYABLE_HTTP_CODES` and `grep -n 'import random'` post-deletion to confirm no surviving references.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `acli.py` import-line removal (acli.py:57, inside the `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import (...)` block) as an explicit edit target in AC item 1, alongside the `__all__` removal.
findings.finding: The AC omits the `acli.py` import line (line 57, inside the `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import (...)` block) as an explicit edit target. The remediation section mentions it, but AC checklist item 1 only says 'removed from acli_subprocess.py and acli.py `__all__`' — an implementer reading only the AC could remove the `__all__` entry and leave the dead import in place, causing an `ImportError` at module load time once `_call_with_backoff` is deleted from `acli_subprocess.py`.
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC item 1 to: '`_call_with_backoff` is removed from `acli_subprocess.py`, from the `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import (...)` block in `acli.py` (line 57), and from `acli.py __all__` (line 98).'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Remediation/AC to reflect that the test can only be REMOVED (not retargeted to `_run_acli`), since `_run_acli` has no `urllib.error` retry path and there is no live equivalent to exercise.
findings.finding: The plan's AC/Remediation asserts the sole test can be 'retargeted to the live `_run_acli` path', but `_run_acli`'s inline retry loop catches `subprocess.CalledProcessError` and `subprocess.TimeoutExpired` — it does NOT retry `urllib.error.HTTPError` or `urllib.error.URLError` at all. The test exercises urllib.error 503 retries; there is no live retry path for that error domain, making 'retarget' infeasible as written.
findings.location: Remediation section: 'delete or retarget the sole test to the live `_run_acli` path'; AC line: 'The sole test is retargeted to the live retry path or removed'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'retarget the sole test to the live `_run_acli` path' with 'remove the sole test (test_acli_retry_loop_chains_cause in test_error_taxonomy.py); no live retry path exercises urllib.error so retargeting is not viable'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC, e.g.: AC1: `grep -rn '_call_with_backoff' src/ acli.py` returns zero matches; AC2: `python -m pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_error_taxonomy.py -v` passes (or the file is absent); AC3: `git diff HEAD -- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_subprocess.py | grep -E 'schedule|retry_count|max_attempts'` returns no additions.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is given for any of the three acceptance criteria — an executor cannot produce verifiable evidence of completion without guessing the right grep/test commands.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to the Acceptance Criteria section:
```
Proving commands:
  AC1: grep -rn '_call_with_backoff' src/ acli.py  # must return zero matches
  AC2: python -m pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ -v  # must pass with no skips on the deleted/retargeted test
  AC3: git diff HEAD -- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_subprocess.py | grep -E 'schedule|retry_count|max_attempts'  # must show no additions
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default decision for the test: either 'prefer retargeting to `_run_acli` unless that path is already covered by existing tests, in which case delete' or 'delete unconditionally' — do not leave the choice open to the executor.
findings.finding: The plan says 'delete or retarget the sole test to the live _run_acli path or removed' without stating a default decision, leaving a real design choice to the executor.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'delete or retarget the sole test to the live `_run_acli` path or removed' with: 'If `_run_acli`'s retry loop is not already covered by an existing test, retarget the test to exercise it; otherwise delete the test file entirely.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan to the Remediation section (e.g., revert commit, or an expand-contract phase where the symbol is deprecated before deletion) and make passing the importer grep a documented prerequisite gate.
findings.finding: The plan removes a publicly re-exported symbol (`_call_with_backoff` in `acli.py __all__`) — a destructive, irreversible operation for external consumers — but provides no explicit rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequencing. The `cascade_flag` note acknowledges the risk ('grep importers') but acknowledgement alone is insufficient per T4.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Rollback section: 'If post-deletion CI or integration checks reveal an unaccounted importer, revert via `git revert <sha>`; the deletion is safe to proceed only after the importer grep returns no hits outside `test_error_taxonomy.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a remediation step (or AC) specifying the action when grep finds importers beyond acli.py and the unit test (e.g., 'if any found, raise a separate deprecation ticket before deletion proceeds').
findings.finding: The cascade_flag instructs 'grep importers before removal' but neither the Remediation steps nor the ACs specify what action to take if grep reveals additional external importers of the publicly re-exported symbol beyond acli.py and the unit test — leaving a silent gap in caller acknowledgment.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Remediation: 'If grep finds importers other than acli.py and test_error_taxonomy.py, do NOT delete yet — open a deprecation ticket and coordinate removal separately.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
